title: Epicharis triangularis

intro: Epicharis triangularis, also known as Bitter Balsam, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its yellow sap and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Epicharis triangularis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Epicharis triangularis is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as being used topically for wound healing and skin care.
